links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: leucogaster |url=https://americanornithology.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/2024-B-final.pdf |website=American Ornithological Society |access-date=17 October 2024}} It is native to the east and central Pacific Ocean

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Cocos booby are found in the East Pacific Ocean, breeding in Costa Rica, Nicaragua, Mexico, French Overseas Territories, Colombia, and the United States. It is also found offshore of Guatemala, El Salvador, and Panama

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: In recent years, Cocos booby has dispersed and is now found in the Central and West Pacific in Japan and Hawaii where they have now bred. They are only recent resident breeders in California, first breeding in the Channel Islands in 2017, and now regularly occur as far north on the Pacific coast as Vancouver. Large colonies exist on the islands of Clipperton Island, San Pedro Mártir Island, Isla Espíritu Santo, Isla Isabel, Islas Marietas, Socorro Island, Isla del Caño, Gorgona Island, and Cocos Island
